programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

The contestants are Carol Decker, Dennis Seaton, Bob Champion, Jo Durie, Samantha Fox, Duncan Norvelle, John Kettley and Lizzie Webb(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Etihad Stadium, Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium, Southampton v Chelsea at St Mary's Stadium and Newcastle United v Manchester United at St James' Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: NFL Highlights
programme.desc: Chicago Bears v Oakland Raiders. Action from the inter-conference clash, as Tottenham Hotspur Stadium hosted an NFL match for the first time(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville present the consumer programme investigating issues around the UK, showing viewers how to make informed choices(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties in West Bromwich, Leeds and Croydon, all bought without viewing ahead of the auction, are sold under the hammer, and the owners reveal whether they regret their buys(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for one of the UK's wealthiest drug smugglers leads officers to Greece, while police in London struggle through rain and snow to track two men wanted in Poland(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: There is an angry confrontation in a London motorcycle shop when enforcement officers try to remove goods to collect a debt owed to a customer(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara toys with Daniel's feelings after their indiscretion, while Karen has hopes for Imogen and Sid. A simple procedure is made complicated by the patient's lies(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett in which contestants must answer questions from nine categories, so must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. The first contestants are a creative writing student, a financial blogger, and a primary school teacher(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin returns with the antiques-buying challenge in which people are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown. In the first edition, hairdresser Sarah faces off against civil servant Marika in Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts transform and then sell three things that were about to be dumped at a tip. Sarah Moore searches for useful items at Wallyford Recycling Centre, taking away a redundant desk, a tired armchair and sheets of corrugated iron but(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Alex Iszatt finds out how digital fingerprinting is being used to fight crime, while Sean Fletcher reveals how a new generation of keepers is bringing back the British bee(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia is stunned to find Martin at Bex's hospital bedside and gives him a few home truths about not being there until now. Desperate for cash, Rainie begs Kathy for a job in the cafe, but Ian is reluctant to even give her the time of day. Bailey asks Karen to be her legal guardian, Daniel and Jean grow even closer and Arshad talks to Mariam about her behaviour toward Iqra(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Myles Bonnar investigates the multimillion-pound industry that sells men pick-up techniques to get women into bed as quickly as possible. He goes undercover as a student in one such group, and discovers an emphasis on getting from first encounter to sex as quickly as possible that crosses lines regarding consent(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contenders begin finals week, with the first challenge involving making two different types of canape in volume. They then head to Tenerife to recreate popular Canary Island dishes to serve to the locals, before working under acclaimed chef Erlantz Gorostiza at Restaurant Martin Berastegui at the Ritz Carlton. The celebrities have to quickly get their head around his cutting-edge gastronomy and techniques as they will each be cooking a dish for the President of the Council of Tenerife and guests(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Friendship, loyalty, laughter and lots of mischief bring Cork inner-city teenagers and best friends Conor and Jock into conflict with their headmaster - leaving Conor's mother to pick up the pieces. To add fuel to the fire, the lads strike up friendships with the headmaster's daughters. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock's friendships with Principal Walsh's daughters intensify, causing awkward tension between the lads, the principal, and Linda and Siobhan themselves. Mrs Walsh is intent on her husband giving the lads a chance, but despite everyone's best intentions, things get out of hand when the duo crash a Walsh family barbecue. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville host the consumer affairs show investigating issues around the UK, showing viewers how to make informed financial choices(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a quirky terraced house in Birmingham, a flat in a Glasgow high rise block, and a former taxi cab office in Upper Holloway(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Officers in London and Derby search for people wanted across Europe, while detectives from south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Some unusual possessions could be used to settle a man's debts to his ex-wife, who is facing bankruptcy. In Cornwall, a sheriff goes after money owed to a boating company(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Lewes, East Sussex, as experts Philip Serrell and Nick Hall help two teams to find three items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Imogen gathers support for the arts festival, and two wily pensioners give Al the runaround. Guest starring Wendy Craig and Bruce Montague, stars of 1970s sitcom Butterflies(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: A customer advisor, an actor and a retired police officer must choose between combining their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Rob Beckett hosts(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin introduces a London couple to his home town of Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, taking them on a trip to the nearby Grand Union Canal and showcasing the local craft ales(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin, in which a marketing expert and a saleswoman are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team goes salvaging at Llanfoist Recycling Centre, and transforms an old wooden door and a radiator, as well as making lights from a collection of discarded bottles(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben gets a threatening call to say Lola has been kidnapped, and in his panic he insists on Martin helping him, agreeing to let Stacey come home if they get Lola back. Habiba confronts Honey about Adam's behaviour, telling her she deserves better, while Mariam apologises to Iqra and assures her she is proud of her. Rainie sees a glimmer of hope when Max asks her to watch Abi and with Bailey missing, the Taylors rally round to find her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric tries to keep his diagnosis - and the life-or-death decision it has presented him with - a secret from Darla, who is wrestling with a tough choice of her own. Essie is confronted by Isla's angry father, who demands to know the identity of her real mother and threatens to expose the illegal adoption. Kian discovers one of his patients is an old flame, but their unresolved feelings for each other may threaten his judgement(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The mysterious Jessica Mallory arrives in London as the most closely guarded secrets of the intelligence services face exposure. Rachel faces a terrible choice that will not only decide Shaun's fate but also have an impact on the nation's entire criminal justice system. Famke Janssen (X-Men) guest stars in the conclusion of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Aspiring neurosurgeon Howra stitches a patient's skull together after brain surgery, while on the night shift, Sofia's training is put to the test as she verifies a death and deals with a patient who is in severe pain. Nick questions his abilities after failing to catheterise two patients, while second-year junior doctor Luke is also feeling nervous about treating patients independently(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation has called stress the 'health epidemic of the 21st century', and it has been blamed for contributing to illnesses such as diabetes, dementia and types of cancer. Here, Fiona Phillips explores scientific research into stress and demonstrates a number of techniques designed to decrease common factors. She also investigates whether it is inherently a bad thing, joining a number of volunteers who put themselves into a number of high-stress situations to truly understand its meaning and how people could use it to their advantage(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don celebrates the berries of the season and plants up potfuls of colour for spring. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the 17th staging of the event, as thousands of runners line up in front of Cardiff Castle to tackle the flat course. A strong African showing is expected, with Wilson Chebet, John Lotiang and Shadrack Kimining set to compete in the men's race, and Paskalia Kipkoech, Lucy Cheruiyot and Lydia Mathathi among the entrants in the women's competition. Commentary comes from John Rawling and Tanni Grey-Thompson, with reports by Lowri Morgan and Jamie Baulch(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote d'Azur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-winning romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: A revealing and fascinating insight into this troubled legend of cinema, who appeared as comfortable in romantic comedies as he was in iconic Hitchcock thrillers. Featuring newly discovered footage shot by Grant himself and readings from his unpublished autobiography, spoken by actor Jonathan Pryce(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils. Action from Semi-Final 2 at DW Stadium, as the sides battled it out for a place in the Grand Final(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 10th and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Norwegian teenage sensation Jakob Ingebrigtsen is expected to go toe-to-toe with Kenya's Timothy Cheruiyot in the 1500m final, with Great Britain's Charlie Da'Vall Grice also hoping to be involved. The men's 10,000m also takes place, and the women's long jump and men's javelin finals get under way in the field. With analysis from Paula Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics: World Championships
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further coverage of the 10th and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. A new champion will be crowned in the 100m hurdles, as 2017 winner Sally Pearson has since retired. The championships then conclude with the women's and men's 4x400m finals, in which Great Britain will be hoping for a strong showing after earning respective silver and bronze medals two years ago(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas, including an eco-friendly range of body and skincare products, a divisive savoury snack, and a brand of fruit-flavoured water. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Unsure until they share their responses, Tom and Louise discuss the rekindling of their relationship. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sheepdog trainer Katy invites three potential partners to experience a taste of country life in the Cumbrian Fells. While the weather is bleak, painter and decorator John, motorbike champion David and former footballer Carl take to herding sheep and mucking out kennels with remarkable success. Grace takes a day off from her family's dairy to visit a hobby farmer on his own turf, while Sara Cox pays a visit to Cumbrian farmer David to deliver letters from six candidates for a speed dating session(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Wallasey, Wirral in Merseyside, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The owners of three detached properties in York compete to be chosen for the final, featuring a Victorian cottage, a 1960s farmhouse and a 1980s bungalow(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe is interviewed over her assault, while Ange battles to keep Evan alive, but with so many conflicting versions of events, it remains uncertain if the truth will out. Cameron is furious, and determined to go to any lengths to see justice is done. Ric tries to support his granddaughter while secretly arranging an MRI scan to uncover the cause of his declining cognitive abilities(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for a cocaine smuggler in the north-west of England leads to an arrest in a wealthy suburb of Accra in Ghana(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A crew of RNLI volunteers in Newquay is called out to a shipwreck off the Cornish coast, where a diver who has lost his mouthpiece needs rescuing from 30 metres underwater. A yacht in the Dover Strait starts to sink - and may collide with a cargo ship before going under - and a Dorset crew search for a windsurfer who has got caught in a storm(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House-proud couples from the Wirral and Lancashire put their homes and lifestyle under the microscope as they exchange properties with complete strangers, before reporting afterwards on how they found the experience. Presented by Giles Brandreth(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Friends of a glamorous south London woman who once travelled the world, call in the probate detectives to try to find her family. Meanwhile, the search for relatives of a silk weaver's son is jeopardised by a surprise revelation. Plus, details of two unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: An exploration of the history and culture of some of the most extreme locations in Britain. Nick Crane visits the Isles of Scilly, Ruth Goodman walks the dangerous path traversed daily by the Branscombe Cliff farmers in Devon, and Mark Horton investigates the Transatlantic Telegraph service. Meanwhile, Hermione Cockburn learns how the fossil of a large sea creature found at St David's Head, Pembrokeshire, shaped scientists' understanding of Earth's history(n)

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The extraordinary animals and people who make their homes in mountain ranges around the world, beginning with the Rockies. The mountains are the spine of North America, a beautiful wilderness of snowcapped peaks and hidden valleys, home to many animals including cougars, wolves and grizzly bears(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Jonathan Pratt find a collection of objects at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, and Paul Martin takes a ride along the Thames on an amphibious vehicle(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Return of the quiz in which four famous faces take part in a week of trivia-based games, beginning with Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend. Plus, interviews with the remaining pairs(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which famous faces take part in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge. In the first edition, tennis coach Judy Murray and former British number one Greg Rusedski compete, with expert guidance coming from Angus Ashworth and Raj Bisram(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as rugby players the Eggchasers take on James Bond fans the 007s. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of The Inheritors, You, The Silence Breakers, and The Protester(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the 11th of the academic quiz's first-round matches, four students from Open University pit their wits against a team from the University of Huddersfield in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: What Britain Buys and Sells in a Day
programme.desc: The team looks at the seafood trade, with Ed Balls finding out how the cargo hub at Heathrow is a crucial link in Britain's supply chain and going behind the scenes of the airport operations centre to witness how delays can affect it. Cherry Healey is in the Highlands to find out why the world is so fond of salmon farmed there and fishes for langoustine on Loch Fyne, while Ade Adepitan travels to Iceland to catch one of Britain's favourite type of fish and unravels the story of the Cod Wars(n)

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As the school year begins, a new mum at the gates attracts the attention of Julia, Liz and Kevin, with Amanda telling them she is a high-flying businesswoman with five kids and a sexy husband. Meanwhile, Julia is struggling to keep her head above water at work when she is offered the choice of promotion or redundancy. Return of the comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As the semi-finals get under way, the remaining three contestants go head to head for the first time as they are challenged to redesign an entire hair salon, with industry experts Kelly Hoppen, Sophie Robinson and Abigail Ahern on hand to offer the designers their advice. With just two days to complete this ambitious challenge, the three designers head to sunny Whitstable on the Kent coast to visit the salons for the first time. One designer decides to go for an ambitious theme with sophisticated lighting scheme, while another opts for bold colours and brave design choices. In the third salon, the competitor decides to play it safe, but will their salon have enough ambition to impress the judges and earn a place in the final? Judging the finished salons alongside Michelle Ogundehin this week is design legend, Lawrence Llewelyn-Bowen(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley is in Cumbria to explore the Lake District's wild side. meeting a man who runs pony treks that follow old pack-horse routes across the fells. Joe then hears more about the region's industrial heritage as he meets artists behind a new installation celebrating the area's copper mining history. Plus, Charlotte Smith investigating the rise of far-right extremism in the countryside(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts transform and then sell three things that were about to be dumped at a tip. Sarah Moore searches for useful items at Wallyford Recycling Centre, taking away a redundant desk, a tired armchair and sheets of corrugated iron but(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for one of the UK's wealthiest drug smugglers leads officers to Greece, while police in London struggle through rain and snow to track two men wanted in Poland(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a 2,000-mile journey along the famous American road, taking them through eight different states, each with their own history, flavours and people. They begin by learning about Chicago's meatpacking industry, before moving on to small-town American, where they spend time with the Amish community, cooking together and learning about their different way of life. Their first leg ends in St Louis, where they try 'concrete custard' - a treat so rich and thick it has to be served upside down(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott serves up a family-sized slab of chocolate recipes from the BBC archives, guaranteed to please the nation's chocoholics. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' classic chocolate pudding, the Hairy Bikers 'world's best' brownies, a chocolate mousse from Raymond Blanc, James Martin's marshmallows with chocolate dipping sauce and a surprise from Nigella Lawson - chocolate pasta(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the women's team final from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany. Despite a number of injury setbacks in recent years, the British team were hoping to reach this final and guarantee their place in the Tokyo Olympics next summer as a result. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the RAF Museum at Hendon in London, where items up for auction include a nautilus shells and some Superman film memorabilia. With antiques experts Michael Baggott and Mark Stacey(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their general knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal chats to the couple that survived the weekend's Dance Off, and takes a closer look at the weekend's dances. Plus, more exclusive interviews and backstage gossip(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kelly Brook and Denise Van Outen compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge, setting out on a trip around south-east England in classic cars. They receive expert guidance from Charles Hanson and Tim Medhurst as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox delivers a collection of love letters to George, a 24-year-old vet in Hay-on-Wye who has not had a relationship in more than two years. She helps him to arrange a speed dating evening with the writers of his favourite letters - but meeting them face to face proves nerve-racking. Dairy farmer David invites three potential partners to stay with him in Cumbria so they can get a taste of rural life. Plus, an update on Martin and Grace, to see how they are getting on in their dating journeys(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: An unusual series of incidents occurs on the London underground, and Will and Caroline find themselves lumbered with representing a man involved. As they delve deeper into the case, they find him difficult to get a handle on, and struggle to decide if he is just a harmless eccentric, or something much creepier. Will takes the opportunity to get to know his mentor a little better - and soon regrets it. Meanwhile, Ashley begins making some political moves. Legal sitcom, starring Will Sharpe and Katherine Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the fifth round of fixtures, including Green Bay Packers v Dallas Cowboys(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a pioneering team in Brazil as they track individual wild jaguars in order to gain new insights into their lives. However, when two young cubs are orphaned, the team must use its new-found knowledge to help them return to the wild(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Film-maker Chris Terrill captures an intimate and personal portrait of life on board ultra-modern super aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth, as its crew learns the intricacies of a warship that will change the way Britain goes to war. In this first instalment, early 2016 sees sailors begin to arrive in Rosyth dockyard in Scotland where the ship is still in construction, but nearing completion. Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors are assembling to take the prototype warship to sea for the first time. These trials herald what should be 50 years of service(n)

programme.title: Jumbo: The Plane That Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins and development of the Boeing 747, which was the world's first wide-bodied commercial airliner and revolutionised air travel after coming into service in 1970. In interviews filmed on the original prototype, chief engineer Joe Sutter and his team describe the challenges they faced, from exploding engines to vibrations that threatened to tear the aircraft apart, and test pilot Brien Wygle reveals how on the first flight they hooked up car batteries as back-ups in case of engine failure(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory(n)

programme.title: Operation Jericho
programme.desc: Martin Shaw tells the story of Operation Jericho, a 1944 mission that saw a squadron of RAF Mosquito bombers try to free French Resistance fighters from a prison in Amiens by destroying its walls. He takes to the air to retrace the British pilots' journey and meets survivors and aircrew as he tries to piece together who originally ordered the raid(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Cora pleads guilty to second-degree murder to avoid a trial, but the judge orders a competency evaluation after a call from Ambrose, but Cora still tries to block the investigation as the detective continues to push for answers. Mason struggles to come to terms with secrets from his wife's past(n)

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A chronicle of New York's drag scene in the 1980s, focusing on balls, voguing and the ambitions and dreams of those who gave the era its warmth and vitality(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Film-maker Chris Terrill captures an intimate and personal portrait of life on board ultra-modern super aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth, as its crew learns the intricacies of a warship that will change the way Britain goes to war. In this first instalment, early 2016 sees sailors begin to arrive in Rosyth dockyard in Scotland where the ship is still in construction, but nearing completion. Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors are assembling to take the prototype warship to sea for the first time. These trials herald what should be 50 years of service(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez and reformed forger Shaun Greenhalgh work to recreate ancient works of art, beginning with a jeweled eagle brooch fit for a Visigoth chieftain. They visit an animal sanctuary in Lancashire to draw inspiration a close-up encounter with a bird of prey - but sourcing authentic materials proves tricky(n)

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores how the history of the American Revolution has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years. He uncovers a story from 1835 in which strange creatures were supposedly sighted on the moon and investigates how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In a radical experiment, pioneering scientists from across Europe have come together to use new technology to explore what our world would look like if we could not tell lies. Three British contributors are rigged to make it impossible for them to lie undetected, and are challenged to live for a whole week without telling a single lie. This bold social experiment sets out to discover the role of deception in our lives, and to investigate the impact lying has on our mental state and the consequences of it for our relationships(n)

programme.title: The Story of China
programme.desc: Michael Wood tells the story of China's first great international age under the Tang Dynasty (618-907). He travels along the Silk Road to the bazaars of Central Asia, and into India on the track of the monk who brought Buddhism back to China. In Xi'an, he talks to Chinese Muslims in the Great Mosque and hears the story of the first reception of Christianity in 635. Moving south, he finds out about the beginnings of China as an economic giant, witnesses a lock on the Grand Canal that was built in 605 but still handles 800 barges a day, and tracks the rise of both silk and tea(n)

programme.title: The Lost Libraries of Timbuktu
programme.desc: Aminatta Forna presents a documentary revealing Africa's literary tradition in Timbuktu, where hundreds of thousands of manuscripts have come to light in recent years. From the 13th to the 16th centuries, the city was a renowned seat of learning, and its black African scholars were revered throughout the Islamic world(n)

programme.title: This Green and Pleasant Land: The Story of British Landscape Painting
programme.desc: Artists, critics and academics examine depictions of the British landscape in art. The programme charts the genre's development from the earliest Flemish paintings in the court of Charles I to David Hockney's digital drawings. Featuring contributions by film-maker Nic Roeg, historian Dan Snow and novelist Will Self(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez and reformed forger Shaun Greenhalgh work to recreate ancient works of art, beginning with a jeweled eagle brooch fit for a Visigoth chieftain. They visit an animal sanctuary in Lancashire to draw inspiration a close-up encounter with a bird of prey - but sourcing authentic materials proves tricky(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Fake News: A True Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of dishonest news reporting over the past 200 years. He uncovers a story from 1835 in which strange creatures were supposedly sighted on the moon and investigates how the rivalry between press barons Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst may have contributed to an actual war. The programme also looks at the influence of technology, from early trick photography to modern deepfakes, and features the owner of a Washington pizza parlour who has been on the receiving end of one of the modern world's most infamous conspiracy theories(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Portugal v England (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Estadio do Bonfim in Setubal. While the male Portuguese team are the reigning European and Nations League champions, the women's side are yet to achieve the same level of success, having only once qualified for a major championships. England will enter this match as favourites given they sit some 25 places above their opponents in the world rankings, but they did only manage a narrow 2-1 win when the sides last met in the group stage of Euro 2017(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Mandy McAuley investigates the launch of a renewed campaign of loyalist violence in 1985, particularly in the wake of the Anglo-Irish Agreement, which granted the Republic of Ireland political influence in the North. The programme explores how loyalist groups used intelligence leaks from soldiers and police, and uncovers evidence of MI5 agents operating inside the Ulster Defence Association(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose visits Ecuador to examine the tale of an 18th-century expedition in which a group of French intellectuals set out to determine the shape of the Earth. The mission was beset by problems from the start as disease, disputes and affairs set the explorers on separate paths and, while great discoveries were made, the team was still to face theft, death and despair(n)

programme.title: The Last Explorers
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Japan to uncover how Victorian Thomas Blake Glover helped rebel samurai clans overthrow the country's rulers in the 1860s and lay the foundations for one of the most powerful economies in the world(n)

programme.title: The Worst Witch
programme.desc: The end of year exams are looming and Maud's annoyed that distracted Mildred isn't taking revision seriously. Miss Cackle goes to the cottage in the woods to learn Agatha has escaped. Ethel is desperate to find out the secret, so she does an impersonation spell to transform into Maud, hoping Mildred will open up to her. Mildred and Enid work out Ethel's plan and tell 'Maud' that the big secret is that Mildred is Miss Cackle's niece. Miss Cackle is annoyed and grounds the two of them. Mildred thinks Miss Cackle isn't herself and worries Agatha had returned from the cottage so she flies to Agatha's cottage in the woods. She meets Agatha and Gullet at the cottage and they try to take her powers, but she escapes. Ethel finds the birth scroll just as Miss Gullet and Agatha arrive at the castle. Esmerelda unwittingly invites them in, thinking Agatha is Miss Cackle, and gives up her powers - only to learn she's given them to the wrong sister. Agatha announces to the school that she is in char(n)

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore paintbrushes through discovery, play, song and story(n)

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious(n)

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Sammy solves a splashy problem with water and some pipes(n)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to Africa to meet some meerkats and record their alarm call on his gizmo(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Sinker is off on her holidays so sends strict Captain Captain to keep an eye on Cook and Line. Saeed, Katie, Lucy and Alfie play Stepping Stones and Pirate Cannons before taking on the Shipwreck Rummage(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels accidentally trap Duggee in his workshop while he is having a clear out(n)

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: When HQ gets twisted up, tensions are stretched tight to fix the problem(n)

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Smelly socks and cheesy feet are brie-lliant, thinks Messy, but when it is Lofty's feet that smell, it is a giant problem(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a bowling alley to find out how it works, and we learn how ribbon is made(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie shows off three amazing machines that are used on farms(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Jemima is short on fruit ingredients for her flapjacks, and in a misunderstanding with Peter, she heads to Mr McGregor's garden to find some(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters arrive at the Bonneville Salt Flats in Utah, USA, a huge dry lakebed that is perfect for high-speed driving(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: The Lost Pirate sends a message in a bottle in a bid to escape a desert island, newsreader Arthur Sleep mistakes his diary for a script, and antiques expert Ann Teak gives a masterclass in spotting real china plates(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells spend their first night together as a new family, only to be kept awake by Waffle, whose constant barking upsets Mrs Hobbs, their cat-obsessed neighbour(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly and her friends go litter-picking on the beach. Meanwhile, Moira has lost her precious whistle and no-one realises it's in Molly's bucket and about to get recycled(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After Colly Wobble loses her shows, Moon Baby shows her a magical shoe tree in Storyland(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny hears a strange voice joining in with her singing and discovers a flower singing as she travels through space on a rocky asteroid. Tiny discovers that the flower is lonely, and comes up with a clever plan to help her(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy lets Igglepiggle hold her special stone while she rides the Ninky Nonk, but he forgets all about it(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Edith Bowman reads I Wish I'd Been Born a Unicorn, by Rachel Lyon and Andrea Ringli(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lisa is keen to move abroad for a new life in Australia and away from the stresses of running a village pub to allow for a change of pace. However, the relocation would require her husband James to come to terms with being separated from his daughter, and the family must make their decision after sampling life in the city of Melbourne(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the 17th staging of the event, as thousands of runners line up in front of Cardiff Castle to tackle the flat course. A strong African showing is expected, with Wilson Chebet, John Lotiang and Shadrack Kimining set to compete in the men's race, and Paskalia Kipkoech, Lucy Cheruiyot and Lydia Mathathi among the entrants in the women's competition. Commentary comes from John Rawling and Tanni Grey-Thompson, with reports by Lowri Morgan and Jamie Baulch(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Thomas Plant join two teams as they try to find valuable items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, with two boxing instructors competing against a Texan couple. Meanwhile, Tim Wonnacott visits Ormesby Hall near Middlesbrough(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits St George's cathedral in Hertfordshire to meet members of the UK's Coptic Orthodox community, the roots of which can be traced back to first-century Egypt(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Martel Maxwell meet the new owners of a bungalow in Callington in Cornwall and a terrace in Watford, and hear their plans for their auction buys(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking to move to the rural setting of the Scottish Borders on a budget of £850,000, having lived in the city of Brussels for the last 20 years(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a rural back garden with a steep slope in Somerset, each hoping their plan will make the most of the countryside views(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: A team at the Bristol Upholstery Collective get to work on an old three-piece suite, while woodworking duo Josh and Oli try to make something of an old oak beam(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Street Life
programme.desc: Iolo Williams observes gulls rearing chicks and causing havoc on the streets of Aberystwyth, and on the outskirts of town, badgers are dodging cars on a busy main road as they set off on a nightly expedition for food in the town's housing estates. In Conwy, jackdaws have set up home on the castle walls. Pembroke Castle too has its jackdaws, and a beautiful mill pond that attracts otter families looking for eels(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1980s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are Carol Decker, Dennis Seaton, Bob Champion, Jo Durie, Samantha Fox, Duncan Norvelle, John Kettley and Lizzie Webb(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Manchester City v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Etihad Stadium, Arsenal v Bournemouth at Emirates Stadium, Southampton v Chelsea at St Mary's Stadium and Newcastle United v Manchester United at St James' Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.desc: Chicago Bears v Oakland Raiders. Action from the inter-conference clash, as Tottenham Hotspur Stadium hosted an NFL match for the first time(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties in West Bromwich, Leeds and Croydon, all bought without viewing ahead of the auction, are sold under the hammer, and the owners reveal whether they regret their buys(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: The search for one of the UK's wealthiest drug smugglers leads officers to Greece, while police in London struggle through rain and snow to track two men wanted in Poland(n)

The first contestants are a creative writing student, a financial blogger, and a primary school teacher(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin returns with the antiques-buying challenge in which people are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown. In the first edition, hairdresser Sarah faces off against civil servant Marika in Liverpool(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts transform and then sell three things that were about to be dumped at a tip. Sarah Moore searches for useful items at Wallyford Recycling Centre, taking away a redundant desk, a tired armchair and sheets of corrugated iron but(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Down the Social with Owen Money
programme.desc: Mandy Starr and Owen visit the Newbridge Memo, exploring the 1920s art deco theatre and the ballroom where young locals in the 1940s and 50s would come to see dance bands. They talk to a couple who met at the venue half a century ago and still attend dances there today(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia is stunned to find Martin at Bex's hospital bedside and gives him a few home truths about not being there until now. Desperate for cash, Rainie begs Kathy for a job in the cafe, but Ian is reluctant to even give her the time of day. Bailey asks Karen to be her legal guardian, Daniel and Jean grow even closer and Arshad talks to Mariam about her behaviour toward Iqra(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Myles Bonnar investigates the multimillion-pound industry that sells men pick-up techniques to get women into bed as quickly as possible. He goes undercover as a student in one such group, and discovers an emphasis on getting from first encounter to sex as quickly as possible that crosses lines regarding consent(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contenders begin finals week, with the first challenge involving making two different types of canape in volume. They then head to Tenerife to recreate popular Canary Island dishes to serve to the locals, before working under acclaimed chef Erlantz Gorostiza at Restaurant Martin Berastegui at the Ritz Carlton. The celebrities have to quickly get their head around his cutting-edge gastronomy and techniques as they will each be cooking a dish for the President of the Council of Tenerife and guests(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Friendship, loyalty, laughter and lots of mischief bring Cork inner-city teenagers and best friends Conor and Jock into conflict with their headmaster - leaving Conor's mother to pick up the pieces. To add fuel to the fire, the lads strike up friendships with the headmaster's daughters. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock's friendships with Principal Walsh's daughters intensify, causing awkward tension between the lads, the principal, and Linda and Siobhan themselves. Mrs Walsh is intent on her husband giving the lads a chance, but despite everyone's best intentions, things get out of hand when the duo crash a Walsh family barbecue. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy and Chris Walley(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Renee Zellweger discusses playing iconic singer Judy Garland in new movie Judy, while comedian and actor Lenny Henry reflects on his life and times as new memoir is Who Am I, Again? is published. Documentary-maker Louis Theroux promotes Gotta Get Theroux This: My Life and Strange Times in Television, and one-time Wham! member Andrew Ridgeley talks about his life and work with much-missed pop star George Michael(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville host the consumer affairs show investigating issues around the UK, showing viewers how to make informed financial choices(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Officers in London and Derby search for people wanted across Europe, while detectives from south Wales work with Tanzanian police to locate a murderer on the run in Africa(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Some unusual possessions could be used to settle a man's debts to his ex-wife, who is facing bankruptcy. In Cornwall, a sheriff goes after money owed to a boating company(n)

programme.desc: A customer advisor, an actor and a retired police officer must choose between combining their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Rob Beckett hosts(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin, in which a marketing expert and a saleswoman are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team goes salvaging at Llanfoist Recycling Centre, and transforms an old wooden door and a radiator, as well as making lights from a collection of discarded bottles(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben gets a threatening call to say Lola has been kidnapped, and in his panic he insists on Martin helping him, agreeing to let Stacey come home if they get Lola back. Habiba confronts Honey about Adam's behaviour, telling her she deserves better, while Mariam apologises to Iqra and assures her she is proud of her. Rainie sees a glimmer of hope when Max asks her to watch Abi and with Bailey missing, the Taylors rally round to find her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric tries to keep his diagnosis - and the life-or-death decision it has presented him with - a secret from Darla, who is wrestling with a tough choice of her own. Essie is confronted by Isla's angry father, who demands to know the identity of her real mother and threatens to expose the illegal adoption. Kian discovers one of his patients is an old flame, but their unresolved feelings for each other may threaten his judgement(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The mysterious Jessica Mallory arrives in London as the most closely guarded secrets of the intelligence services face exposure. Rachel faces a terrible choice that will not only decide Shaun's fate but also have an impact on the nation's entire criminal justice system. Famke Janssen (X-Men) guest stars in the conclusion of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Aspiring neurosurgeon Howra stitches a patient's skull together after brain surgery, while on the night shift, Sofia's training is put to the test as she verifies a death and deals with a patient who is in severe pain. Nick questions his abilities after failing to catheterise two patients, while second-year junior doctor Luke is also feeling nervous about treating patients independently(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation has called stress the 'health epidemic of the 21st century', and it has been blamed for contributing to illnesses such as diabetes, dementia and types of cancer. Here, Fiona Phillips explores scientific research into stress and demonstrates a number of techniques designed to decrease common factors. She also investigates whether it is inherently a bad thing, joining a number of volunteers who put themselves into a number of high-stress situations to truly understand its meaning and how people could use it to their advantage(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: Documentary following officers from Britain's biggest and busiest police force as they deal with life, death, crime and its victims across London. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Leicester City at Anfield and Brighton & Hove Albion v Tottenham Hotspur at AMEX Stadium. After missing out on the title by one point last season, the Reds look set to challenge for the title once again, but were held to a 1-1 draw by the Foxes here last term. Meanwhile, Spurs will be hopeful of another victory over the Seagulls after doing the double over them in the 2018/19 campaign. Plus, Burnley v Everton, Norwich City v Aston Villa, Watford v Sheffield United and West Ham United v Crystal Palace(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a rural back garden with a steep slope in Somerset, each hoping their plan will make the most of the countryside views(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in Buckinghamshire to learn about the history of the harvest festival and the traditions surrounding it - from the introduction of the threshing machine to the origins of the corn dolly. There are also films from the programme's archive about harvests, including Matt Baker finding out about Jersey Royal potatoes still being unearthed in the same way they were 200 years ago(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. Plus, singer and actor Harry Connick Jr performs Cole Porter's Broadway classic Anything Goes(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: In devastated Warsaw, Kasia makes plans to flee with her grief-stricken mother Maria, but gets caught in a resistance cell's attack on German soldiers. Harry returns to Manchester, but when he finally plucks up the courage to tell Lois the truth she disarms him by declaring her love, while Tom shocks Douglas by announcing he is now a conscientious objector. In Berlin, Nancy is frustrated with the growing power of the censor, while Webster and Albert's joy is overshadowed by the growing threat of prejudice they face(n)

programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. programme.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (2001)
programme.desc: An orphaned boy endures a miserable childhood, until he discovers he has magical powers and enrols at a special school for young wizards. However, his suspicions are aroused by a sinister teacher who seems to be up to no good. Fantasy adventure, adapted from the first of JK Rowling's books, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson, Robbie Coltrane, Alan Rickman and Richard Harris(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: There's an alert at HMP Downview women's prison when repeat offender Collette O'Flaherty, distraught at missing out on a place at the prisoners' family day, takes a fellow inmate hostage. A prisoner at HMP Portland hears false rumours that he is a sex offender - and suspects the culprit spreading the lies is a new officer. The inmates at HMP Bullingdon look forward to Friday, when they receive small treats like chocolate and shower gel - but dealing in these items quickly leads to violence(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Steve Fulcher is lauded by the media for leading an investigation that recovered the bodies of two women murdered by Christopher Halliwell, but behind the scenes faces a disciplinary hearing at the hands of the IPCC, charged with gross misconduct. Karen Edwards refuses to give up her fight to get justice for her daughter Becky, and goes to meet with her local MP Robert Buckland to discuss a change that she thinks needs to made to the Police and Criminal Evidence Act. Starring Martin Freeman and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: A Confession

However, fresh hope arises when Wiltshire Police find a way to possibly bring Christopher Halliwell to court for Becky's murder - but the disgraced detective must return home to face the man who ended his career(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Serial Killer - with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Documentary about Steve Fulcher, the detective superintendent who destroyed his own career in his determination to catch suspected killer Christopher Halliwell. Having arrested the taxi driver on suspicion of killing Sian O'Callaghan, Fulcher bonded with the suspect, who confessed to killing Sian and a second murder years before. But the detective's failure to caution Halliwell and denying him access to a solicitor meant the evidence was inadmissible(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants who have never met before pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Liam has a moment of inspiration, Tracy misreads a situation, and Vanessa's anger bubbles over(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Sean Fitzpatrick, Sam Warburton and Paul O'Connell, reports from Gabriel Clarke and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and David Flatman(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: France v Tonga (Kick-off 8.45am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of both teams' third and penultimate Pool C match, held at Kumamoto Stadium in Japan. With analysis from Sean Fitzpatrick, Paul O'Connell and Sam Warburton, and commentary by Martin Gillingham, Scott Hastings and Alan Quinlan(n)

programme.title: Free Willy 2: The Adventure Home (1995)
programme.desc: The friendly whale is reunited with Jesse, the boy who saved him from captivity. Jesse and his younger half-brother Elvis take time out from a family holiday to help save Willy and his pod from the danger posed by oil spilling from a crippled tanker. Family adventure, starring Jason James Richter, Michael Madsen, August Schellenberg and Francis Capra(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (2001)
programme.desc: An orphaned boy endures a miserable childhood, until he discovers he has magical powers and enrols at a special school for young wizards. However, his suspicions are aroused by a sinister teacher who seems to be up to no good. Fantasy adventure, adapted from the first of JK Rowling's books, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson, Robbie Coltrane, Alan Rickman and Richard Harris(n)

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: There's an alert at HMP Downview women's prison when repeat offender Collette O'Flaherty, distraught at missing out on a place at the prisoners' family day, takes a fellow inmate hostage. A prisoner at HMP Portland hears false rumours that he is a sex offender - and suspects the culprit spreading the lies is a new officer. The inmates at HMP Bullingdon look forward to Friday, when they receive small treats like chocolate and shower gel - but dealing in these items quickly leads to violence(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

programme.title: A Confession
programme.desc: Steve Fulcher is lauded by the media for leading an investigation that recovered the bodies of two women murdered by Christopher Halliwell, but behind the scenes faces a disciplinary hearing at the hands of the IPCC, charged with gross misconduct. Karen Edwards refuses to give up her fight to get justice for her daughter Becky, and goes to meet with her local MP Robert Buckland to discuss a change that she thinks needs to made to the Police and Criminal Evidence Act. Starring Martin Freeman and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: To Catch a Serial Killer - with Trevor McDonald

Having arrested the taxi driver on suspicion of killing Sian O'Callaghan, Fulcher bonded with the suspect, who confessed to killing Sian and a second murder years before. But the detective's failure to caution Halliwell and denying him access to a solicitor meant the evidence was inadmissible(n)
