programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: When Sharon and Tracey discover Dorien has been keeping a secret for more than 50 years, they decide to get involved, but the novelist is reluctant to confront her past. Meanwhile, Tracey finds motherhood isn't all it's cracked up to be. Sitcom, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is nominated by Auntie Vera to take over her Pearly Queen title, but when Sharon finds out, she tries to outperform her sister to bag the role for herself. Meanwhile, Dorien reluctantly agrees to a family reunion. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Troubled saleswoman Helen (Nicola Walker), whose mother was murdered back in episode one, turns up on Janet's doorstep with information that takes the investigation in a completely different direction - paving the way for the formidable Det Supt Julie Dodson to be called in. Meanwhile, new recruit Sgt Rob Waddington arrives on the scene - putting Janet's nose out of joint as she is demoted back down to constable. Emmerdale star Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken offers Deirdre financial help but she refuses it, Jack treats Vera to a day in Blackpool, and Rita is annoyed by the way Jenny takes advantage of Mavis(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey believes Alf cannot live without the shop, while Liz and Jim call a family conference, and then spend the evening together(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Sarah argue about what they should tell Annie about the farmhouse. Eric's attempts at reconciliation with Elizabeth are not working(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Archie is upset at the way Zoe has treated him, Frank is furious with Joe, Chris warns Joe to stay away from Kim, and Annie feels bitter towards her family(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and the inspector try to fix a water heater, but their efforts end in disaster. Comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby's divorce finally comes through, and he wastes no time in proposing to Miss Jones - but makes the mistake of asking his low-life brother Ron to be best man. Final episode of Eric Chappell's classic comedy, which finally reveals Rigsby's Christian name. Leonard Rossiter stars(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth has second thoughts about visiting Michael in prison. Alan returns from his holiday. Kathy tells Jack that Annie is angry with the family about how she has been treated(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is hooked on Robert's new games console. Frank causes a scene in front of a client and throws Joe out, reminding him that he has been sacked. Mark invites Debbie on a date(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is thrilled after his night with Liz, while Colin resigns from the pub after finding out what she did. Tracy packs her bags and leaves home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is stunned to find Tracy has gone, and Ken is horrified at the thought of her living with a 22-year-old. Amy asks Liz to give Andy her blessing(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Having realised her marriage is a mistake, Rachel is still staying at Janet's and making excuses for her absence, but Sean isn't prepared to accept that things have gone so wrong, so quickly. Meanwhile, the investigation into Joe Bevan continues as the body count rises and the team faces the mammoth task of identifying the victims and determining the order in which they were murdered. Detective drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Sean Maguire(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Dorothy becomes concerned her grand-daughter Taisie is being influenced by Rachel, while Janet is mortified when Gill berates her for inappropriate behaviour toward young sergeant Rob. The detective duo are sent to investigate the death of a man in a nursing home after his daughter complains about the level of care he had been receiving. Will they find any evidence of foul play? Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Danny Miller(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Newly promoted DS Hazel Wallace returns from training to investigate when a man is found hanged - a case further complicated by the fact that, according to police records, the victim actually died a year previously. It is not long before Jack is back from his extended compassionate leave to help her sort out the whole sorry mess. David Jason stars, with Caroline Harker, Kate McEnery and Cheryl Campbell(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger joins forces with Mim to tackle a double challenge - the threat of a game being called off due to rain, and a challenge to his leadership from another member of the team. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Easter arrives and everyone prepares for the race, but Rachel, Sangeeta and Jayesh go missing on the fells. Frank causes trouble in the Woolpack when he sees Kim and Joe together(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe plans to sue Frank for unfair dismissal. Jack warns Joe not to interfere in farm business. Kim finds out that Neil Kincaid has been blocking her business dealings(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jenny knows Robert took Mitzi, and Rita confronts Jenny about her lies. Ken urges Tracy not to hurt Deirdre. Phyllis takes advantage of Percy's jealousy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily helps Bernard organise a car boot sale. Jim tells Denise he does not know where he stands with Liz. Ken meets Craig at Tracy's flat(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes the matron and an obnoxious doctor may have conspired to commit murder. Whodunit, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey cuts the canteen's opening hours, but when he sacks one of the staff, things really turn nasty. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A local nightclub owner is admitted with symptoms of poisoning, and his son is quick to point the finger of suspicion at his father's glamorous wife. Frankie is shocked to see Dr Ormerod doubt himself as they tend a drunken farmer after a tractor accident, while Dr Goodwin deals with a case of domestic abuse and a stationery salesman takes a shine to Lizzie. Robert Daws and Scott Taylor star(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin is accused of taking sides when he goes too far to help a woman who claims her husband is hitting her, and there is a mystery to solve when a patient is admitted under a false name. Meanwhile, Dr Ormerod's strange behaviour shows signs of becoming worse - and Frankie fears the surgeon is becoming addicted to his medication. Medical drama, with Robert Daws, Wendy Craig and Amy Robbins(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The villagers of Aspern Tallow begin to believe the ghost of a former resident has come back to haunt them. Barnaby and Troy have to determine why packing cases are apparently combusting spontaneously - and whether it is more than a coincidence that the battered body of a relative of the dead man has been found in the family vault. With John Nettles, Prunella Scales and Patricia Brake(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: When Sharon and Tracey discover Dorien has been keeping a secret for more than 50 years, they decide to get involved, but the novelist is reluctant to confront her past. programme.desc: Elizabeth has second thoughts about visiting Michael in prison. Alan returns from his holiday. Kathy tells Jack that Annie is angry with the family about how she has been treated(n)

programme.desc: Jack is hooked on Robert's new games console. Frank causes a scene in front of a client and throws Joe out, reminding him that he has been sacked. Mark invites Debbie on a date(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Outside Edge

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.desc: Emily helps Bernard organise a car boot sale. Jim tells Denise he does not know where he stands with Liz. Ken meets Craig at Tracy's flat(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes the matron and an obnoxious doctor may have conspired to commit murder. Whodunit, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. programme.title: Columbo: The Conspirators (1978)

Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Clive Revill, Jeanette Nolan and Bernard Behrens(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade continues his investigations into supposedly deadly freshwater creatures. From right across the Amazon there are stories of a colossal fish that is said to swallow men whole. Some call it the 'lau-lau', others the 'piraiba'. But after 20 years of criss-crossing South America, Jeremy has never seen a specimen big enough to convince him that the fishermen's tales are true. In this programme, he heads to a remote and little-known backwater of Guyana on a mission to finally track down this alleged monster(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off

Second World War adventure, starring Clint Eastwood, Telly Savalas, Donald Sutherland, Don Rickles and Harry Dean Stanton(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Conclusion. Despite his reservations, Hornblower is reluctant to dishonour his bride-to-be by leaving her standing at the altar. However, with the ink barely dry on the marriage certificate, a surprise guest at the wedding breakfast despatches the groom to France on a mission to uncover the whereabouts of a missing sloop and its captain, Bracegirdle. En route, Hornblower rescues two strangers caught in a storm. Historical Navy drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Paul McGann and Lorcan Cranitch(n)

programme.title: American Sniper (2014)
programme.desc: A Navy Seal becomes a legend to his colleagues thanks to his skills as a deadly sniper. On his return to the US following four tours of duty in Iraq, he finds living an ordinary life as a husband and father far more difficult to deal with than the demands of warfare. Biopic based on the experiences of real-life Iraq conflict veteran Chris Kyle, directed by Clint Eastwood and starring Bradley Cooper, Sienna Miller and Kyle Gallner(n)

programme.desc: Doyle and Bodie pose as hitmen to infiltrate a mysterious group of hire killers whose targets are all retired government officials, and supposedly no longer of any strategic importance. Adventure, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the river, and how it has played a pivotal role in London's development as a major centre for international trade. The first episode focuses on the capital's docklands, which at their peak, employed more than 30,000 workers. A boat captain whose family have hauled cargo along the river for six generations recalls his grandfather's unique talents. Plus, a look at the construction of bridges, sewers, and the flood barrier(n)

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: Tracy Borman goes in search of the lost royal palace of Henry VIII, a sumptuous group of royal buildings that were once part of the Tower. The lovesick king spent a fortune on building them to celebrate his marriage to Anne Boleyn, only to destroy all memories of her after her execution(n)

programme.title: Doing Drugs for Fun?
programme.desc: Troi and Chanel witness the unsanitary conditions under which cocaine is made, and discover the ingredients that go into the drugs they routinely use. Louis encounters a cocaine smuggler and Amber accompanies armed officers in the Colombian Navy on duty(n)

programme.title: Murdered By
programme.desc: The 2013 murder of Georgia Williams, a policeman's 17-year-old daughter and Army cadet who was tortured on camera by a friend and shop worker with fantasies about snuff movies(n)

programme.title: Crack in the World (1965)
programme.desc: A dying scientist and his colleagues conduct experiments to harness the Earth's inner energy that seem set to end in disaster. Sci-fi thriller, with Dana Andrews, Janette Scott, Kieron Moore and Alexander Knox(n)

programme.title: Stuart Little 2 (2002)

Family adventure sequel, with Geena Davis, Hugh Laurie and Jonathan Lipnicki, and the voices of Michael J Fox, Melanie Griffith and James Woods(n)

programme.title: Eragon (2006)
programme.desc: A young farm boy discovers a dragon's egg, which hatches into the last surviving specimen of its mighty race. His friendship with the winged creature soon comes in handy when he discovers he is destined to overthrow the evil tyrant who has taken control of the kingdom. Fantasy adventure, starring Ed Speleers, Jeremy Irons, Robert Carlyle and Sienna Guillory, with Rachel Weisz providing the voice of the dragon(n)

programme.title: The River Wild (1994)
programme.desc: A headstrong white-water rafting expert embarks on a holiday with her family, but their fun comes to an abrupt end when two killers show up. The fugitives' only escape route from the law is down a raging river, so they take the woman and her son hostage to act as guides. Meanwhile, her husband desperately tries to keep track from the riverbank, waiting for his opportunity to launch a rescue attempt. Action thriller, starring Meryl Streep, Kevin Bacon, David Strathairn, Joseph Mazzello and John C Reilly(n)

programme.title: X-Men: The Last Stand (2006)
programme.desc: The mutant heroes return to face a battle on three fronts. Arch-enemy Magneto is back with an army of followers, while a corporation has created a drug that permanently robs mutants of their powers. Meanwhile, their former ally Jean Grey has returned from the dead as the destructive and seemingly unstoppable Phoenix. The third instalment of the superhero saga, starring Hugh Jackman, Ian McKellen, Famke Janssen, Halle Berry and Kelsey Grammer(n)

programme.title: Dodgeball: A True Underdog Story (2004)

Sporting comedy, starring Ben Stiller, Vince Vaughn and Christine Taylor(n)

programme.desc: Two men make a perilous journey from Burkina Faso to Italy in search of a better life for them and their families. Despite surviving desert bandits, a treacherous sea voyage and arrest, nothing can prepare the two men for the hostility and violence that awaits them in Europe. Drama, starring Alassane Sy and Koudous Seihon. In French, Italian, English and Arabic(n)

programme.desc: Apache chief Cochise resolves to make a treaty allowing his people to live in peace with white settlers. However, his hot-headed fellow leader Geronimo disagrees, and puts the negotiations at risk by embarking on a murder spree, incurring the wrath of his enemies. Western, with John Lund, Jeff Chandler and Susan Cabot(n)

programme.title: 7th Cavalry (1956)
programme.desc: An army officer is branded a coward for failing to fight in the Battle of the Little Bighorn, and is unable to convince anyone that official business had detained him. In a bid to repair his damaged reputation, he leads a cavalry detail to retrieve the dead from the battlefield. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Jay C Flippen and Jeanette Nolan(n)

programme.title: The Tall Stranger (1957)
programme.desc: A Union soldier struggles to recover from a violent encounter with a gang of lawless cattle rustlers, but his recuperation is cut short as he works to ensure the safety of a group of Confederate homesteaders journeying north at the end of the Civil War. Western based on a novel by Louis L'Amour, starring Joel McCrea, Virginia Mayo, Michael Ansara and Michael Pate(n)

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones(n)

programme.title: The Book Thief (2013)
programme.desc: A girl separated from her family is raised by foster parents in Nazi Germany, who instill in her a love of reading. She is horrified by the book burnings and tries to save volumes from the flames, while she and her adoptive family provide a safe hiding place for a Jewish boy. Drama based on Markus Zusak's novel, starring Sophie Nelisse, Geoffrey Rush and Emily Watson(n)

programme.desc: Disillusioned British Muslim Omar decides to form a terrorist cell, but the only recruits he can find are his dim-witted friend, an angry and bitter white convert and a timid bomb-maker. The group's incompetent attempts to strike a blow against Western society culminate in a scheme to attack the London marathon. Chris Morris' satirical comedy, with Riz Ahmed and Kayvan Novak(n)

They shut themselves in a rural mansion for the months-long and ancient Abramelin Procedure, which includes days without sleep, fasting, backbreaking rites and ritual sex - and cannot be abandoned safely before its completion. Psychological horror, starring Catherine Walker and Steve Oram(n)

programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover(n)

Thirty-two-year-old account manager Kieron from Basingstoke has spent the past three years single to focus on his career, but now he's ready to put finding a partner at the top of his to do list. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Funeral service manager Peter Pickering hosts the second dinner party in Northamptonshire, hoping his traditional approach will win him the £1,000 cash prize. However, one guest does not like bread and butter pudding, and Stephanie is horrified by Jules's shocking revelation(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: As the third party from Northamptonshire gets under way, stay-at-home mum Stephanie Reeder's nerves are evident as she plans to serve classic fish and chips, while offering a glamorous clothes-swapping session for entertainment(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business planning manager Lauren Oxlade is the penultimate host in Northamptonshire. programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge helps new owners Erin and JB, who have discovered rising damp after recently moving into their fairy-tale chateau, complete with turrets and a moat(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eccentric sheepdog trainer Katy Cropper hosts the third dinner party in Cumbria, where she prepares 'come a cropper' prawns al fresco, opens a bottle of Champagne with a Masai sword, and serves some potent home-made horseradish sauce. Aside from the food, a sheep-herding demonstration proves to be a hit with her guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holly Venning hosts the fourth dinner party from Cumbria and the food marketing manager wants to show her love for her home county through her menu. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Wendy Tinnion helms the final event in Cumbria and decides to throw a Friday night disco for her fellow diners. However, her guests are in for a shock when they discover something unexpected about their host(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy(n)

The keepers are concerned when a newborn foal takes a turn for the worse, and a Sulawesi macaque monkey Rumple gives birth to a baby called Rey, only for the baby to get bullied by some of the higher-ranking females(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman and her sons bring in their springer spaniel puppy for complicated surgery on its badly deformed hind leg. The owner of an eight-year-old cocker spaniel waits to hear if her dog's cancer has spread, and the vet treats a two-year-old cat, which is dragging one of its back legs(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In Maidstone, retired couple Bill and Jean Letley plan a highly contemporary bungalow complete with underfloor heating and prototype steel foundations. The project would test even the most accomplished workmen, but is left to their daughter and son-in-law, who have little building experience and have to make personal sacrifices(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: Inside Cadbury: Chocolate Secrets Unwrapped
programme.desc: Documentary examining the world of one of Britain's biggest and best-loved chocolate makers. Behind closed doors at Cadbury's Bournville plant, a team of scientists struggle to meet the biggest challenge the company has faced for a century, to dramatically cut sugar from their Dairy Milk recipe. Can the new bar match up to its beloved predecessor?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Peter, 85, has been brought - unwillingly - to A&E by his stepson Brian. He cut his finger on some wire two weeks earlier and his hand is swollen and won't stop bleeding. Emma, 23, is rushed to St George's after crashing her car on the way to her father's house, while doctors also see to 86-year-old Harry, who fell down a flight of stairs a day earlier but didn't seek treatment. Ciaran, 27, injured his ankle the previous night while out with friends and was given a temporary cast. Emergency nurse practitioner Helen needs to get him X-rayed and replastered, but he faces a return visit for an operation(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Lynn and Brett Merritt from Hampshire fell in love with laid-back Cape Verde the moment they stepped off the plane on their first visit, and have been dreaming of buying a holiday home there ever since. Jasmine Harman shows them five properties on this stunning speck in the Atlantic within their £55,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp meet the Pogsons in Harrogate, North Yorkshire and offer advice to the family who are unsure whether to renovate their property or find a new home. After living in their three-bedroom house for 16 years, Carol thinks that it is time for a big change, but Andrew is adamant he is not moving house(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time actress Caroline Nash is the host on day three of the competition in Northampton, and she hopes that a menu based on her travels will dazzle her guests. However, the night gets off to a shaky start as Donna Harris finds a hair on the side of her goat's cheese salad, while businessman Duncan Fielding seems more interested in goats themselves than the food(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Barb Wiles hosts the fourth dinner party in the Northampton area, and hopes her Australian-themed menu will wow her guests and win her the £1,000 prize. However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole refurbishes a classic Ford Mustang and a neon garage sign, while Simon O'Brien restores a classic Chopper bicycle and turns a bass guitar into a chair(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Two of the young Asian elephants contract the herpes virus, leaving the keepers doing everything they can to save the animals' lives, using anti-viral medication and a pioneering blood transfusion. When silvery gibbon Tilu gives birth, dad Alven has to keep their older son, tearaway Eko, in check. The keepers wonder why jaguars Napo and Goshi have not mated after six years together, and a new species of cricket arrives at the insect house(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Londoner Chris Ostwald decides to build a New England-style water mill in the Chilterns, on a hill and nowhere near any water, after being inspired by an illustration on a yoghurt pot. He bought the plot 20 years previously and it has always been his dream to build a retreat for himself and his family. Everything seems to be going smoothly, until a neighbour complains the house is breaking its planning consent. Kevin McCloud presents(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

The basement is the latest room to be transformed, serving as a nerve centre for the whole chateau, a space for storing deliveries, and Dick's workshop. The old piggery is also renovated, in anticipation of some new arrivals(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Despite the best attempts of paramedics and the crew of the Wiltshire Air Ambulance, the injuries sustained by one of the victims prove fatal. As Wiltshire Police launch a full-scale murder investigation to find the perpetrator, all is not as it may first have seemed in a case that raises difficult questions about why people carry knives, and how they should be punished for doing so(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to the Lake District to look for copper, a forgotten piece of the nation's industrial heritage. There are nearly two dozen old mines across the mountains, some thought to have been established 400 years ago when the valleys would have been studded with workshops, scaffolding and water-powered machines, home to a brave band of Tudor miners. The team battles the rain, wind and dangerously unstable trackways, but the combination of sheer effort and hi-tech kit enables the experts to access the heart of an old mine(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team heads to the small county of Rutland to visit Oakham Castle, the most well-preserved 12th-century building in Britain. It was once home to the Norman knight Walkelin de Ferrers, who fought alongside Richard the Lionheart in the Crusades, and before that the residence of several Saxon kings and queens. Tony and the crew investigate de Ferrers' long-lost private quarters, but it takes three days of twists and turns to determine what the site looked like 900 years ago. To mark the visit, they also cast a giant Time Team horseshoe to add to the collection that has decorated the castle hall for half a millennium(n)

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Faye Dunaway(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder, Smoke and Shadows (1989)
programme.desc: An ambitious Hollywood director is visited by an old friend, who threatens to expose evidence of his involvement in the death of an actor. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan(n)

Columbo suspects the victim's identical twin nephews - a flamboyant TV chef and a conservative banker - who stand to inherit his fortune if he died unmarried, but the detective is unsure which of them is the guilty party. Drama, starring Peter Falk, and Martin Landau in a dual role. With Paul Stewart, Julie Newmar and Jeanette Nolan(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: Chicago Fire

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: The stories of a teenage shop assistant who fought back against an armed robber, and a fly-tipper who got the shock of his life when he was caught doing his dirty deed(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood recounts the story of a barman who had three armed men enter his club and demand to be taken to the safe. Plus, a man whose beloved garden bench was stolen(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Monkey World rescue centre in Dorset. Alison Cronin retrieves a squirrel monkey that has become aggressive and started chewing its tail after living alone for 20 years in a cage in Essex. Back at base, a delivery of mangoes proves popular among the chimps, and the team learns of a forthcoming arrival(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison Cronin and her colleagues prepare to transport an overweight orang-utan from South Africa back to the Monkey World rescue centre in Dorset. The task poses plenty of logistical challenges, but matters are further complicated when the experts discover the creature has developed unusual habits(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The experts face unexpected problems at the airport as they prepare to fly Oshine to the UK, leaving them struggling to keep the overweight orang-utan calm. Back at Monkey World, a baby chimpanzee tries to take advantage of its privileged position and capuchin monkeys get to grips with frozen fruit(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Oshine has an eventful first night in the orang-utan nursery, and Jeremy Keeling realises helping the new arrival settle in will not be a straightforward task. There is also exciting news at the woolly monkey house, and Hanyana and the chimpanzees are released into their revamped enclosure for the first time(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A member of staff acts as a surrogate parent for Mikado, an orphaned golden-cheeked gibbon that has been brought to the centre from France. The chimpanzees play with laundry at the nursery, while recently arrived squirrel monkey Fidget makes an impression on the residents(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Jeremy is forced to step in at the orang-utan nursery when new arrival Oshine gets a frosty reception from A-mei, and baby gibbon Mikado settles into his new home with the help of primate care staff member Mike, acting as his surrogate mum. The chimps get a fruity surprise and there is exciting news at the woolly monkey house(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Tensions are high as Sammy the chimp, one of the park's oldest and most popular residents, goes into surgery. The stump-tailed macaque house gets a makeover to accommodate its elderly monkeys and the capuchins go cherry-picking(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Orang-utan Oshine gets weighed, having been on a strict diet since arriving from South Africa - but she is reluctant to climb on the scales and insists that Jeremy gets on too. Meanwhile, baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado is introduced to his new foster mother Alex and Bart proves to be a star pupil(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison collects 10 stump-tailed macaques being retired from a research facility in Edinburgh. The journey is potentially dangerous as the stumpies have never been outside before and some of them are old and frail. Back at the park, staff are anxious as chimp Athena undergoes an eye operation, while squirrel monkey Fidget is a hit with the ladies(n)

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Staff prepare for the arrival of two new woolly monkeys from Switzerland, but they are worried how the animals will cope with the long journey - and how they will fit in once they get here. The male capuchins are put to the test with a clever puzzle feeder and the female stump-tailed macaques vie for superiority(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012(n)
